site.name: Generational Commonwealth
site.subtitle: Official Constitutional Portal
site.constitutionTitle: The Constitution of the Generational Commonwealth
site.description: A formal public portal for the constitutional institutions, agencies, articles, and national identity of the Generational Commonwealth.

articles.title: Status, Interpretation, and Constitutional Purpose
articles.summary: Section 1 — Nature of This Instrument This Constitution establishes a fictional democratic constitutional commonwealth organized around distributed executive authority, generational representation, nonpartisan public elections, measurable administration, protected civil liberty, local productive capacity, technological advancement, material circulation, infrastructure resilience, and the preservation of society during catastrophic disruption.
sections: V. The Assembly of State Delegations
VII. The Judiciary and Constitutional Balance
IX. The Office of National Performance and Accountability
excerpt: Section 1 — Nature of This Instrument

This Constitution establishes a fictional democratic constitutional commonwealth organized around distributed executive authority, generational representation, nonpartisan public elections, measurable administration, protected civil liberty, local productive capacity, technological advancement, material circulation, infrastructure resilience, and the preservation of society during catastrophic disruption.
Section 2 — Controlling Principles
1. Sovereignty originates in the people and is delegated only through this Constitution.
2. No generation, official, institution, corporation, donor, military body, algorithm, or emergency authority may possess complete control of the Commonwealth.

articles.title: Preamble and Foundational Order
articles.summary: We, the people of the Commonwealth, recognizing that liberty is endangered by concentrated power, that generations possess different experiences but equal dignity, that government promises must be measured against actual results, and that civilization must be prepared both to advance and to endure, establish this Constitution. We establish a republic in which no generation governs alone; no public action escapes record and review; no private wealth purchases public authority; no technology advances without regard.
excerpt: We, the people of the Commonwealth, recognizing that liberty is endangered by concentrated power, that generations possess different experiences but equal dignity, that government promises must be measured against actual results, and that civilization must be prepared both to advance and to endure, establish this Constitution.

We establish a republic in which no generation governs alone; no public action escapes record and review; no private wealth purchases public authority; no technology advances without regard for human welfare; no useful material is treated as disposable by default; no essential system is permitted to remain needlessly fragile; and no emergency is permitted to destroy the constitutional order it claims to preserve.

The purposes of this Commonwealth are liberty, justice, public safety, equal protection, productive prosperity, local resilience, scientific advancement, human dignity, intergenerational fairness, and the continuous capacity to restore and improve civil society.

articles.title: Fundamental Rights and Liberties
articles.summary: Section — Freedom of Religion and Conscience Government shall establish no official religion and shall neither favor nor burden religious belief or nonbelief. No religious test shall be imposed for public office, public employment, voting, education, public benefit, or civic participation.
sections: Section — Freedom of Religion and Conscience
Section — Freedom of Speech and Expression
Section — Right to Keep and Bear Arms
Section — Security of Person, Home, Property, Communications, and Data
Section — Due Process and Criminal Procedure
excerpt: Section — Freedom of Religion and Conscience

Government shall establish no official religion and shall neither favor nor burden religious belief or nonbelief. No religious test shall be imposed for public office, public employment, voting, education, public benefit, or civic participation.
Section — Freedom of Speech and Expression

Every person may speak, publish, create, criticize, receive lawful information, and express political, scientific, artistic, academic, religious, and personal views. Law may address direct threats, fraud, criminal solicitation, defamation, unlawful harassment, exploitation, or other clearly defined harmful conduct, but shall not punish lawful criticism of government.
Section — Freedom of the Press

articles.title: The Generational Cohorts
articles.summary: For executive representation, every citizen belongs to exactly one age cohort according to age on the official election qualification date. Cohort membership affects executive voting and constitutionally authorized representative structures only; it shall not limit equal civil rights. Cohort Ages Representative Perspective
excerpt: For executive representation, every citizen belongs to exactly one age cohort according to age on the official election qualification date. Cohort membership affects executive voting and constitutionally authorized representative structures only; it shall not limit equal civil rights.
Cohort Ages Representative Perspective
I 18–27 Education, technological adaptation, entry into work, housing access, and future generations
II 28–38 Families, workforce mobility, entrepreneurship, childcare, and household formation
III 39–49 Production, infrastructure, industrial coordination, and institutional management

articles.title: The Generational Executive Council
articles.summary: Collective Executive Authority Executive authority is vested collectively in five Cohort Executives, one elected by each cohort. Each Executive possesses one equal vote. No Executive may exercise unilateral national authority except where this Constitution expressly permits immediate temporary action.
sections: Ordinary executive action Three of five Executives
excerpt: Collective Executive Authority

Executive authority is vested collectively in five Cohort Executives, one elected by each cohort. Each Executive possesses one equal vote. No Executive may exercise unilateral national authority except where this Constitution expressly permits immediate temporary action.
Decision Required Approval
Ordinary executive action Three of five Executives
Major national program, borrowing, extended Four of five Executives emergency, or foreign military deployment

articles.title: The Assembly of State Delegations
articles.summary: Tripartite State Representation Each state elects a single State Delegation composed of three delegates elected from separate legislative age bands. The delegation acts as one constitutional unit and casts one indivisible state vote.
excerpt: Tripartite State Representation

Each state elects a single State Delegation composed of three delegates elected from separate legislative age bands. The delegation acts as one constitutional unit and casts one indivisible state vote.
Delegate Age Band
Emerging Delegate 18–34
Established Delegate 35–54

articles.title: The Judiciary and Constitutional Balance
articles.summary: Constitutional Supreme Court The highest court shall contain fifteen members selected to provide professional, generational, regional, and intellectual diversity.
sections: Civic member 1 Demonstrated public-service or civil-liberties experience
excerpt: Constitutional Supreme Court
The highest court shall contain fifteen members selected to provide professional, generational, regional, and intellectual diversity.
Category Seats Qualification
Senior judges 4 At least fifteen years of judicial experience
Practicing lawyers 3 At least twelve years of legal practice

articles.title: National Action Records
articles.summary: Every significant law, executive order, regulation, contract, infrastructure project, grant, tax change, emergency action, military action, public-benefit program, agency reorganization, continuation, renewal, or action materially affecting constitutional rights must possess a National Action Record before taking effect, except for immediate defensive emergencies. Emergency records must be published within seventy-two hours. Plain Language and Length
excerpt: Every significant law, executive order, regulation, contract, infrastructure project, grant, tax change, emergency action, military action, public-benefit program, agency reorganization, continuation, renewal, or action materially affecting constitutional rights must possess a National Action Record before taking effect, except for immediate defensive emergencies. Emergency records must be published within seventy-two hours.
Plain Language and Length
Record Type Maximum Length Purpose
Short-form action 750 words or 3 pages Limited actions with established methods and low complexity
Standard action 2,500 words or 10 pages Ordinary major laws, programs, regulations, and projects

articles.title: The Office of National Performance and Accountability
articles.summary: The Office of National Performance and Accountability, known as ONPA, independently measures whether public actions produce promised outcomes at reasonable cost and time without imposing unacceptable burdens upon other cohorts, institutions, communities, or future generations. ONPA may audit, inspect, model, investigate, publish, and trigger review, but may not enact policy, command agencies, prosecute crimes, or remove elected officials. Cohort Performance Index
excerpt: The Office of National Performance and Accountability, known as ONPA, independently measures whether public actions produce promised outcomes at reasonable cost and time without imposing unacceptable burdens upon other cohorts, institutions, communities, or future generations. ONPA may audit, inspect, model, investigate, publish, and trigger review, but may not enact policy, command agencies, prosecute crimes, or remove elected officials.
Cohort Performance Index
Measure Weight
Policy effectiveness 25%
Implementation efficiency 20%

articles.title: Expiration, Review, and Closure of Public Actions
articles.summary: Every government program, regulation, authority, appropriation, and administrative action expires. Permanent programs are prohibited except where expressly created by this Constitution. Indefinite, continuing, automatic, or equivalent language shall not evade expiration.
excerpt: Every government program, regulation, authority, appropriation, and administrative action expires. Permanent programs are prohibited except where expressly created by this
Constitution. Indefinite, continuing, automatic, or equivalent language shall not evade expiration.
Action Type Maximum Initial Duration
Emergency authority 30 days
Temporary regulation 1 year

articles.title: The National Revenue Service and Public Finance
articles.summary: All national government income shall be collected through the National Revenue Service, which replaces the former federal income-tax administration system. Government agencies may not independently solicit or accept private donations, sponsorships, naming rights, conditional grants, or privately earmarked contributions. Administration
sections: Every dollar above $10,000,000 100% Social Support Contribution
excerpt: All national government income shall be collected through the National Revenue Service, which replaces the former federal income-tax administration system. Government agencies may not independently solicit or accept private donations, sponsorships, naming rights, conditional grants, or privately earmarked contributions.
Administration
43. Automatic calculation and completed preliminary returns where possible
44. No annual filing requirement for fully documented persons with zero liability
45. Individual rather than household taxation

articles.title: The Local Productive Economy, Employment Registry, and Consumer Commerce
articles.summary: prohibiting lawful interstate or international commerce. Community Economic Registries
sections: Durable Product Standard and Right to Repair
excerpt: prohibiting lawful interstate or international commerce.
Community Economic Registries

Every municipality and state shall maintain an Economic Registry recording aggregate population, legitimate available jobs, filled jobs, occupations, compensation ranges, required skills, working hours, workforce shortages, housing availability, students, retirees, caregivers, persons seeking work, persons medically limited or unable to work, and public-support needs. A national system aggregates local data into a National
Employment Exchange.

No public registry shall disclose an individual’s medical condition, disability, income, benefits, employment status, or other private information. Economic inactivity alone is not a crime. Persistent unexplained nonparticipation may trigger an offer of assessment, job matching, training, education, transportation, housing help, rehabilitation, treatment, or public service. No person may be compelled to accept private employment.

articles.title: Subscription Continuity and Tier Non-Regression
articles.summary: continuing to provide a materially equivalent service. Existing subscribers retain the agreed price unless they voluntarily change plans, terminate service, or the provider lawfully discontinues the product under this Article. Higher Tiers
excerpt: continuing to provide a materially equivalent service. Existing subscribers retain the agreed price unless they voluntarily change plans, terminate service, or the provider lawfully discontinues the product under this Article.
Higher Tiers

A higher-priced tier may be created only when it offers substantial new products, services, functionality, content categories, capacity, or measurable consumer benefit. Advertisement removal, restoration of removed features, ordinary technical quality, basic security, bug fixes, customer service, or artificially imposed restrictions do not independently constitute material additional value.
Non-Regression Once Subscribed

Once at least one person subscribes to a defined tier, the provider may not materially reduce, degrade, restrict, or remove the features, service quality, access conditions, content categories, advertising conditions, storage, performance, device access, or principal benefits included when that subscriber enrolled.

articles.title: Technological Advancement and Human Safety
articles.summary: The National Agency for Technological Advancement and Human Safety shall accelerate technology that measurably improves scientific capability, prosperity, productivity, human welfare, medicine, infrastructure, or national resilience while identifying and reducing physical, psychological, developmental, economic, environmental, and societal harms. Neither advancement nor safety automatically dominates; the objective is maximum longterm societal benefit consistent with liberty.
sections: T0 Minimal societal risk No intervention
T2 Significant population exposure Mandatory impact reporting
excerpt: The National Agency for Technological Advancement and Human Safety shall accelerate technology that measurably improves scientific capability, prosperity, productivity, human welfare, medicine, infrastructure, or national resilience while identifying and reducing physical, psychological, developmental, economic, environmental, and societal harms.
Neither advancement nor safety automatically dominates; the objective is maximum longterm societal benefit consistent with liberty.
Freedom of Innovation
No technology may be prohibited merely because it is new, disruptive, economically transformative, politically controversial, or inconsistent with existing institutions.
Restrictions require specific evidence of substantial harm or a substantial probability of harm and must use the least restrictive effective means.

articles.title: Material Circulation, Resource Recovery, and the Elimination of Landfills
articles.summary: National Principle No material product may enter commerce without an approved pathway for continued use, maintenance, repair, reuse, redistribution, disassembly, component recovery, material recovery, recycling, biological processing, industrial conversion, or secure temporary containment. Disposal is treated as a failure of design, production, collection, or administration.
sections: Central Materials Recovery and Recycling Authority
M00 Direct reuse and standardized components
excerpt: National Principle

No material product may enter commerce without an approved pathway for continued use, maintenance, repair, reuse, redistribution, disassembly, component recovery, material recovery, recycling, biological processing, industrial conversion, or secure temporary containment. Disposal is treated as a failure of design, production, collection, or administration.
Central Materials Recovery and Recycling Authority

The Central Materials Recovery and Recycling Authority establishes national standards for material identity, composition reporting, disassembly, collection, transport, sorting, cleaning, reuse, remanufacturing, recycling, biological processing, hazardous recovery, unresolved containment, and redistribution. It maintains the National Material Circulation
Database and the National Material Bucket System.

articles.title: Homeland Infrastructure Stability and Civilization Continuity
articles.summary: National Duty The Commonwealth, states, municipalities, public authorities, and critical operators shall maintain permanent capability to preserve essential human needs and minimum civil order during severe disruption. Critical systems shall be designed for survivability, redundancy, decentralization, repairability, reserve capacity, manual fallback, rapid restoration, and degraded operation, not merely lowest cost or ordinary efficiency.
sections: National Continuity and Infrastructure Stability Authority
excerpt: National Duty

The Commonwealth, states, municipalities, public authorities, and critical operators shall maintain permanent capability to preserve essential human needs and minimum civil order during severe disruption. Critical systems shall be designed for survivability, redundancy, decentralization, repairability, reserve capacity, manual fallback, rapid restoration, and degraded operation, not merely lowest cost or ordinary efficiency.
Essential Continuity Systems

Essential systems include drinking water, water treatment, sewage, wastewater, electricity, fuel and gas, internet and telecommunications, emergency communications, hospitals, emergency medical services, fire and rescue, essential medicines, food production and distribution, critical transportation, payment systems necessary for essential commerce, material recovery required for safety, and any system whose prolonged failure threatens life, health, civil order, or national survival.
National Continuity and Infrastructure Stability Authority

articles.title: National Survival Mode and the Preservation of Civil Society
articles.summary: Purpose and Activation National Survival Mode is the final constitutional apparatus for conditions in which ordinary government, infrastructure, commerce, communications, healthcare, food distribution, public safety, or administration can no longer maintain minimum national survival. Recession, protest, political opposition, labor action, criticism, electoral instability, or ordinary crime do not independently justify activation.
excerpt: Purpose and Activation

National Survival Mode is the final constitutional apparatus for conditions in which ordinary government, infrastructure, commerce, communications, healthcare, food distribution, public safety, or administration can no longer maintain minimum national survival. Recession, protest, political opposition, labor action, criticism, electoral instability, or ordinary crime do not independently justify activation.
Activation requires four of five Cohort Executives, two-thirds of State Delegations, emergency review by the Constitutional Supreme Court, and a Survival Declaration Record.
If communication makes approval temporarily impossible, four Executives may activate the mode for no more than seven days.
Survival Levels

articles.title: Public Budget Transparency, Lobbying, and Ethical Government
articles.summary: Every public expenditure must trace to a lawful revenue source, legislative appropriation, National Action Record, responsible agency, responsible official, stated purpose, expiration date, and review schedule. Budgets shall be published in human-readable, machinereadable, transaction-level, and simplified forms. Secret appropriations are prohibited; classified spending remains subject to independent audit and judicial review.
excerpt: Every public expenditure must trace to a lawful revenue source, legislative appropriation,

National Action Record, responsible agency, responsible official, stated purpose, expiration date, and review schedule. Budgets shall be published in human-readable, machinereadable, transaction-level, and simplified forms. Secret appropriations are prohibited; classified spending remains subject to independent audit and judicial review.
Petition Without Purchased Access

People and organizations retain the right to petition government through documented public channels. Private paid access is prohibited. Substantive communications concerning legislation, regulation, procurement, taxation, appointments, contracts, enforcement, and
National Action Records must be disclosed. Advocates may submit evidence and testimony but may not provide officials money, gifts, services, travel, employment promises, or other benefits.

articles.title: Public Algorithms and Computational Transparency
articles.summary: No algorithm used for taxation, benefits, enforcement prioritization, elections, resource allocation, performance scoring, employment access, medical treatment, legal status, or constitutional accountability may operate as a secret system. 91. Purpose and legal authority
excerpt: No algorithm used for taxation, benefits, enforcement prioritization, elections, resource allocation, performance scoring, employment access, medical treatment, legal status, or constitutional accountability may operate as a secret system.
91. Purpose and legal authority
92. Inputs and outputs
93. Decision logic
94. Source code where legally permissible

articles.title: Intergenerational Equity and Constitutional Stewardship
articles.summary: Government shall evaluate how actions distribute costs, benefits, risks, debt, environmental consequences, infrastructure obligations, technological harms, material burdens, and institutional effects across generations. No cohort may permanently transfer unreasonable costs to another cohort or future generations without explicit disclosure, supermajority authorization where required, and independent review. Candidate public records shall preserve historical performance, including policies sponsored, results,.
excerpt: Government shall evaluate how actions distribute costs, benefits, risks, debt, environmental consequences, infrastructure obligations, technological harms, material burdens, and institutional effects across generations. No cohort may permanently transfer unreasonable costs to another cohort or future generations without explicit disclosure, supermajority authorization where required, and independent review.

Candidate public records shall preserve historical performance, including policies sponsored, results, failures, cost variance, forecast accuracy, rights effects, and intergenerational impact. Public service records may not be erased, concealed, or materially altered.

articles.title: States, Municipalities, and Distributed Administration
articles.summary: States and municipalities retain authority not delegated to the Commonwealth or prohibited by this Constitution. Local government shall administer community services, economic registries, Continuity Cells, public safety, infrastructure operations, material collection, local courts, and other lawful functions consistent with national rights and interoperability standards. National standards may establish minimum rights, safety, transparency, circulation, continuity, and accountability requirements. Local.
excerpt: States and municipalities retain authority not delegated to the Commonwealth or prohibited by this Constitution. Local government shall administer community services, economic registries, Continuity Cells, public safety, infrastructure operations, material collection, local courts, and other lawful functions consistent with national rights and interoperability standards.

National standards may establish minimum rights, safety, transparency, circulation, continuity, and accountability requirements. Local governments may exceed those standards where they do not violate constitutional rights or obstruct lawful interstate activity.

articles.title: Emergency Powers and Constitutional Continuity
articles.summary: Emergency authority must be specific, publicly justified, geographically and substantively limited, reviewable, and automatically expiring. Emergency declarations shall identify the condition, affected area, activated powers, rights effects, responsible officials, review dates, and termination conditions. No emergency suspends the Constitution. Courts, succession, public records, distributed facilities, emergency communications, offline law access, and elections shall be preserved.
excerpt: Emergency authority must be specific, publicly justified, geographically and substantively limited, reviewable, and automatically expiring. Emergency declarations shall identify the condition, affected area, activated powers, rights effects, responsible officials, review dates, and termination conditions.
No emergency suspends the Constitution. Courts, succession, public records, distributed facilities, emergency communications, offline law access, and elections shall be preserved.
Defensive action, continuity operations, and Survival Mode are governed by Articles IV,
XVI, and XVII.

articles.title: National Defense and Civilian Control
articles.summary: Military authority remains subordinate to civilian constitutional government. Offensive war requires unanimous Executive Council approval and two-thirds of State Delegations. Defensive action may begin under the higher executive threshold established by Article IV but expires without legislative authorization.
excerpt: Military authority remains subordinate to civilian constitutional government. Offensive war requires unanimous Executive Council approval and two-thirds of State Delegations.
Defensive action may begin under the higher executive threshold established by Article IV but expires without legislative authorization.
Domestic military support may provide logistics, engineering, transportation, medicine, communications, rescue, infrastructure repair, and protection of critical facilities.
Domestic law enforcement by military forces shall be narrowly limited, temporary, and judicially reviewable.

articles.title: Public Office, Fiduciary Duty, and Personal Accountability
articles.summary: Every official is a public fiduciary and must act for the public benefit rather than personal, commercial, familial, organizational, or factional advantage. Financial interests, gifts, outside positions, professional relationships, and conflicts must be disclosed. Deliberate obstruction of lawful auditing, falsification of public data, concealment of material risk, bribery, profiteering, and theft of public or survival resources are constitutional offenses. Corporate or public office does not shield a person who.
excerpt: Every official is a public fiduciary and must act for the public benefit rather than personal, commercial, familial, organizational, or factional advantage. Financial interests, gifts, outside positions, professional relationships, and conflicts must be disclosed. Deliberate obstruction of lawful auditing, falsification of public data, concealment of material risk, bribery, profiteering, and theft of public or survival resources are constitutional offenses.

Corporate or public office does not shield a person who knowingly directed, authorized, concealed, or substantially enabled serious misconduct. Penalties remain proportionate and subject to due process.

articles.title: Human Support, Medical Incapacity, and Social Dignity
articles.summary: The Commonwealth shall maintain a humane system for aged persons, disabled persons, medically unable persons, persons with severe cognitive impairment, and persons temporarily unable to sustain themselves because of catastrophic illness or injury. Eligibility shall be medically and administratively reviewable without degrading treatment or public disclosure of private conditions.
excerpt: The Commonwealth shall maintain a humane system for aged persons, disabled persons, medically unable persons, persons with severe cognitive impairment, and persons temporarily unable to sustain themselves because of catastrophic illness or injury.
Eligibility shall be medically and administratively reviewable without degrading treatment or public disclosure of private conditions.

Support may include basic income, housing, medical care, long-term care, assisted living, home health, caregiver support, rehabilitation, mobility, nutrition, transportation, and accessibility. Programs remain subject to National Action Records, expiration, effectiveness review, fraud safeguards, and constitutional appeal.

articles.title: Implementation, Measurement, and Constitutional Simulation
articles.summary: Before full implementation, major institutional mechanisms may be tested through transparent simulations, pilot programs, constitutional stress tests, and controlled regional trials. Simulation shall not substitute for democratic authorization or be represented as certainty. The national constitutional simulation shall model citizens, cohorts, candidates, State
excerpt: Before full implementation, major institutional mechanisms may be tested through transparent simulations, pilot programs, constitutional stress tests, and controlled regional trials. Simulation shall not substitute for democratic authorization or be represented as certainty.
The national constitutional simulation shall model citizens, cohorts, candidates, State
Delegations, Executives, courts, agencies, policies, National Action Records, taxes, material flows, technological progress, infrastructure dependencies, continuity conditions, and
Survival Mode. Models, assumptions, source code, data provenance, confidence limits, and known omissions shall be publicly documented.

Simulation results shall be used to identify deadlock, corruption, capture, fiscal instability, rights infringement, supply failure, cohort conflict, technological debt, material bottlenecks, infrastructure cascades, and restoration weakness. No simulation result alone may enact law or remove a person from office.

articles.title: Amendment and Entrenched Constitutional Guarantees
articles.summary: Amendment Procedure 103. Approval by two-thirds of the Assembly of State Delegations
excerpt: Amendment Procedure
103. Approval by two-thirds of the Assembly of State Delegations
104. Approval by four of the five Cohort Executives
105. Approval by a majority of voters nationwide
106. Approval by a majority within at least four of the five Executive Cohorts

articles.title: Transition, Ratification, and Initial Timetables
articles.summary: General Transition The transition to this Constitution shall preserve ordinary public services and avoid abrupt termination of lawful rights, contracts, benefits, infrastructure operation, and judicial proceedings. Existing institutions continue temporarily to the extent consistent with this
sections: National Revenue Service Operational within three years
excerpt: General Transition

The transition to this Constitution shall preserve ordinary public services and avoid abrupt termination of lawful rights, contracts, benefits, infrastructure operation, and judicial proceedings. Existing institutions continue temporarily to the extent consistent with this
Constitution until successor institutions are established.
Priority Timetables
System Initial Completion Requirement

articles.title: Domestic Human Security, Homelessness Elimination, and Suspension of Foreign Aid
articles.summary: The direct material obligations of the Commonwealth shall be fulfilled toward its own citizens before discretionary public resources are transferred abroad. This Article establishes a measurable domestic sufficiency standard, a permanent homelessnesselimination system, treatment and protective housing for citizens unable to care for themselves, and a constitutional suspension of foreign aid until domestic human security is independently certified. Section 1 — Domestic Priority
excerpt: The direct material obligations of the Commonwealth shall be fulfilled toward its own citizens before discretionary public resources are transferred abroad. This Article establishes a measurable domestic sufficiency standard, a permanent homelessnesselimination system, treatment and protective housing for citizens unable to care for themselves, and a constitutional suspension of foreign aid until domestic human security is independently certified.
Section 1 — Domestic Priority

The Commonwealth shall first secure shelter, food, potable water, sanitation, essential medical care, personal safety, and necessary support for citizens who cannot safely support themselves. Foreign aid shall remain suspended until every state has achieved and maintained the Domestic Human Security Standard.
Section 2 — Foreign Aid Defined

Foreign aid includes grants, subsidized loans, military assistance transferred without full payment, foreign budget support, development assistance, debt forgiveness intended as assistance, public humanitarian transfers abroad, and material support furnished without equivalent compensation. Accounting form, intermediaries, or reclassification shall not defeat this definition.

articles.title: Housing Access, Limited Ownership, Nonprofit Transfer, and Income-Based Occupancy
articles.summary: Housing shall serve first as a place of residence and human stability rather than an unrestricted vehicle for speculation. This Article establishes limits on residential accumulation, a guaranteed path to first ownership, controlled transfer values, nonprofit rental operation, mandatory income-based affordability, and conversion of unused commercial property when lowest-tier housing demand is unmet. Section 1 — Residential Ownership Limits
sections: Certified individual annual income Maximum residential properties
excerpt: Housing shall serve first as a place of residence and human stability rather than an unrestricted vehicle for speculation. This Article establishes limits on residential accumulation, a guaranteed path to first ownership, controlled transfer values, nonprofit rental operation, mandatory income-based affordability, and conversion of unused commercial property when lowest-tier housing demand is unmet.
Section 1 — Residential Ownership Limits
Certified individual annual income Maximum residential properties
Below $500,000 One $500,000 through $1,000,000 Two

articles.title: National Rail Revitalization, Mobility Access, and Transportation Independence
articles.summary: The Commonwealth shall construct a modern national railway network capable of replacing a substantial portion of personal-vehicle commuting and short-distance air travel while connecting every state capital, major population center, regional community, and local feeder system. The system shall be affordable, convenient, domestically maintainable, technologically advanced, and usable during national emergencies. Section 1 — National Rail Authority
sections: Section 7 — Unified Ticketing and Offline Access
excerpt: The Commonwealth shall construct a modern national railway network capable of replacing a substantial portion of personal-vehicle commuting and short-distance air travel while connecting every state capital, major population center, regional community, and local feeder system. The system shall be affordable, convenient, domestically maintainable, technologically advanced, and usable during national emergencies.
Section 1 — National Rail Authority

An independent National Rail Development and Mobility Authority shall plan corridors, build and modernize infrastructure, establish engineering and safety standards, coordinate state and local systems, certify equipment, maintain public rail data, supervise unified ticketing, manage strategic components, and coordinate domestic manufacturing.
Section 2 — State Capital Hubs

Every state capital shall contain a Major State Rail Hub integrating interstate trunk rail, intrastate regional rail, commuter rail, freight where appropriate, buses, municipal transit, bicycle facilities, pedestrian access, airports, ports, and emergency transport. A hub may be distributed across coordinated stations where a single terminal would create excessive congestion or vulnerability.

articles.title: National Education, Child Development, Productive Learning, and School Security
articles.summary: Education shall prepare every child for competent adulthood, scientific and technological participation, skilled work, civic responsibility, personal independence, creativity, physical and mental health, and continued learning. Schools shall function as secure, welcoming, full-workday institutions that support working families without reducing childhood to continuous formal instruction. Section 1 — Universal Educational Access
sections: Section 3 — STEM Priority and Arts Foundation
Section 10 — Continuous Monitoring and Protective Staffing
excerpt: Education shall prepare every child for competent adulthood, scientific and technological participation, skilled work, civic responsibility, personal independence, creativity, physical and mental health, and continued learning. Schools shall function as secure, welcoming, full-workday institutions that support working families without reducing childhood to continuous formal instruction.
Section 1 — Universal Educational Access

Public education, transportation, meals, required supplies, ordinary technology, and workday childcare shall be provided without tuition. Access shall not depend on family wealth, property, disability, geography, family structure, parent citizenship, academic performance, or ability to provide daytime supervision.
Section 2 — Developmental Structure
Education shall progress through Early Childhood, Foundation, Exploratory STEM, and

articles.title: National Health Intelligence, De-Identified Medical Data, and Preventive Care
articles.summary: The Commonwealth shall aggregate de-identified medical knowledge at national scale to improve diagnosis, treatment, prevention, mental health, public-health readiness, and reduction of comorbidities without creating a named national medical surveillance system. Section 1 — National Health Intelligence Network
sections: Section 2 — No Names and Minimal Identifiers
Section 4 — Protected Identifier and Separate Linkage
Section 5 — State Health Data Nodes and Backups
Section 9 — Prohibition of Re-Identification and Commercial Sale
Section 10 — Mental Health and Genetic Protections
excerpt: The Commonwealth shall aggregate de-identified medical knowledge at national scale to improve diagnosis, treatment, prevention, mental health, public-health readiness, and reduction of comorbidities without creating a named national medical surveillance system.
Section 1 — National Health Intelligence Network

A distributed National Health Intelligence Network shall support diagnostic accuracy, treatment comparison, adverse-effect detection, rare-disease identification, comorbidity research, public-health planning, prevention, rehabilitation, physical health, mental health, and development of new medical methodologies.
Section 2 — No Names and Minimal Identifiers

The national analytical system shall not contain names, addresses, telephone numbers, email addresses, government identifiers, financial accounts, employer or school identity, photographs, facial-recognition data, family names, or unnecessary biometrics. The most identifying ordinary fields shall be date of birth, blood type, medically relevant biological characteristics, general geographic region, and a protected nonpublic record identifier.

articles.title: Credit Card Regulation, Consumer Protection, and Debt Relief
articles.summary: Credit cards shall operate as limited payment and short-term borrowing tools, not as permanent systems for extracting interest from consumers unable to reduce principal. This Article prohibits youth revolving debt, unfair interest, manipulative targeting, hidden fees, and indefinite minimum-payment structures while guaranteeing a standardized interestfree repayment path.
sections: Section 4 — Unfair Interest and Penalty Rates
Section 7 — Account Freeze and Recurring Charges
excerpt: Credit cards shall operate as limited payment and short-term borrowing tools, not as permanent systems for extracting interest from consumers unable to reduce principal. This
Article prohibits youth revolving debt, unfair interest, manipulative targeting, hidden fees, and indefinite minimum-payment structures while guaranteeing a standardized interestfree repayment path.
Section 1 — Minimum Age

No person under twenty-five may open, jointly hold, guarantee, or become legally liable on a consumer credit-card account. Persons under twenty-five may use debit, prepaid accounts, non-revolving secured products, or an authorized-user card carrying no personal liability. Campus solicitation and youth-directed marketing are prohibited.
Section 2 — Ability-to-Repay Underwriting

articles.title: National Circular Sanitation, Water Conservation, and Elimination of Conventional Water-Carried Sewage
articles.summary: Clean water is a strategic national resource. The routine use of potable or reusable water merely to transport human waste shall be progressively eliminated. The Nation shall replace the flush, dilute, transport, treat, and discharge model with circular sanitation based on separation, concentration, pathogen destruction, recovery, reuse, resilience, and minimal residual waste. Section 1 — National Circular Sanitation Authority
sections: Section 4 — New Construction and Major Renovation
Section 5 — Water Quality Classes and Lowest Safe Use
Section 8 — Resource Recovery and Sanitation Facilities
Section 10 — Industrial Pretreatment and Contaminant Responsibility
excerpt: Clean water is a strategic national resource. The routine use of potable or reusable water merely to transport human waste shall be progressively eliminated. The Nation shall replace the flush, dilute, transport, treat, and discharge model with circular sanitation based on separation, concentration, pathogen destruction, recovery, reuse, resilience, and minimal residual waste.
Section 1 — National Circular Sanitation Authority

An independent National Circular Sanitation Authority shall establish technical and publichealth standards, coordinate states and municipalities, fund modernization, approve technology, maintain sanitation data, certify facilities, establish contaminant limits, coordinate manufacturing, supervise pilots, enforce milestones, and ensure service continuity.
Section 2 — Public Health First

No conservation, decentralization, recovery, cost savings, or experiment shall justify exposure to untreated human waste, contaminated drinking water or food, uncontrolled pathogens, unsafe indoor conditions, or denial of sanitation. Existing systems shall remain maintained until safe replacements are constructed, tested, staffed, supplied, and certified.

articles.title: Consumer Services, Continuing Payments, Digital Property, and Service Continuity
articles.summary: Consumer services shall remain profitable because consumers continue to choose them and providers continue to deliver promised value. No business shall obtain continuing revenue primarily through confusion, artificial dependency, involuntary renewal, service regression, hidden charges, revocable digital ownership, or destruction of purchased value. Section 1 — Scope and Exclusions
sections: Section 2 — Consumer Service Record and Dashboard
Section 5 — Renewal, Trials, Cancellation, and Dormant Services
Section 6 — Bundles, Embedded Features, and Dependency
Section 8 — No Revocation of Digital Licenses
Section 9 — Offline Access and Local Operation
excerpt: Consumer services shall remain profitable because consumers continue to choose them and providers continue to deliver promised value. No business shall obtain continuing revenue primarily through confusion, artificial dependency, involuntary renewal, service regression, hidden charges, revocable digital ownership, or destruction of purchased value.
Section 1 — Scope and Exclusions

This Article governs subscriptions, memberships, automatic renewals, digital platforms, software and cloud services, streaming, telecommunications, internet access, gaming, data storage, AI services, monitoring, maintenance plans, repair plans, support programs, delivery memberships, fitness and recreation memberships, education services, transportation memberships, connected-vehicle services, recurring deliveries, and similar continuing or access-based consumer arrangements. It does not govern rent, mortgages, lending, credit agreements, insurance, taxes, government fees, court-ordered payments, or ordinary installment purchases resulting in ownership without a continuing service.
Section 2 — Consumer Service Record and Dashboard

Every covered service shall maintain a standardized record stating provider, service, price, billing frequency, enrollment, renewal, benefits, limitations, advertising, data practices, cancellation, refunds, change history, and consent history. An interoperable national dashboard shall allow consumers to view active services, monthly and annualized cost, billing and renewal dates, trials, changes, and cancellation methods without permitting government sale or commercial profiling of preferences.

identity.name: National Identity
identity.source: Article XXXVII, presented here as an identity page rather than an article page.
identity.title: National Identity, Flag, and the American Builder Beaver
identity.summary: The national identity of the Commonwealth reflects construction, continuity, measurement, productive labor, resource stewardship, and resilience.
sections: Section 2 — Meaning of the American Builder Beaver
excerpt: The constitutional order possesses national symbols reflecting construction, continuity, measurement, productive labor, resource stewardship, and resilience rather than conquest or domination.

The American Beaver, Castor canadensis, is designated the national animal of the Commonwealth and may be formally styled the American Builder Beaver in constitutional, ceremonial, educational, and governmental use.
The American Builder Beaver represents practical intelligence, repair, water stewardship, habitat construction, intergenerational work, and patient public resilience.
The beaver may appear on the national seal, educational materials, conservation programs, civic awards, public works insignia, and official ceremonial emblems.
The national flag has a 2:3 proportion, a deep midnight-blue field, five equal white vertical pillars, a silver ring, an eight-point white star, and a red foundation band.
identity.flagMeaning: The flag uses a deep midnight-blue field for continuity and ordered public life, five equal white pillars for the five age cohorts, a silver ring for circular stewardship and institutional continuity, an eight-point white star for common purpose, and a red foundation band for sacrifice and civic duty.
identity.animal: The American Beaver, formally styled the American Builder Beaver, is the national animal and represents construction, patience, practical intelligence, restoration, water stewardship, and commonwealth-building.

institutions.name: Five-Cohort Executive Council
institutions.description: Collective national executive authority, one elected leader per age cohort.

institutions.name: Assembly of State Delegations
institutions.description: Legislative authority organized by state delegations and age-banded representation.

institutions.name: Judiciary and Constitutional Balance
institutions.description: The constitutional judicial branch, including local, appellate, national, and supreme constitutional courts.

institutions.name: State and Local Courts
institutions.description: Trial and local judicial forums within the distributed court structure.

institutions.name: Regional Appellate Courts
institutions.description: Intermediate appellate review across regional jurisdictions.

institutions.name: National High Court
institutions.description: National judicial review beneath the Constitutional Supreme Court.

institutions.name: Constitutional Supreme Court
institutions.description: Final constitutional review and structural balance for the Commonwealth.

institutions.name: National Court for Grave Crimes
institutions.description: Specialized trial forum for nationally grave offenses with full due process and automatic appeal.

institutions.name: States and Municipalities
institutions.description: Distributed administration and retained local authority under the constitutional order.

agencies.name: Office of National Performance and Accountability
agencies.description: ONPA independently measures effectiveness, efficiency, fiscal performance, delivery, transparency, and intergenerational effects.
agencies.articleTitle: The Office of National Performance and Accountability

agencies.name: National Revenue Service
agencies.description: A simplified national revenue institution for individual taxation, public finance, and protected social-support collection.
agencies.articleTitle: The National Revenue Service and Public Finance

agencies.name: Economic Registry
agencies.description: A state and municipal registry for aggregate local productive capacity, employment, commerce, and economic conditions.
agencies.articleTitle: The Local Productive Economy, Employment Registry, and Consumer Commerce

agencies.name: Employment Registry
agencies.description: The employment-facing registry for productive work opportunities, local hiring, and labor-market coordination.
agencies.articleTitle: The Local Productive Economy, Employment Registry, and Consumer Commerce

agencies.name: National Talent Service
agencies.description: A public talent service for linking skills, training, and useful work within the local productive economy.
agencies.articleTitle: The Local Productive Economy, Employment Registry, and Consumer Commerce

agencies.name: National Agency for Technological Advancement and Human Safety
agencies.description: The agency charged with accelerating technological advancement while protecting human safety and child development.
agencies.articleTitle: Technological Advancement and Human Safety

agencies.name: Office of Human Impact
agencies.description: An independent office investigating injury, disease, displacement, dependency, manipulation, or child-development harm from technology.
agencies.articleTitle: Technological Advancement and Human Safety

agencies.name: Central Materials Recovery and Recycling Authority
agencies.description: The authority establishing material recovery standards, producer responsibility, reuse, recycling, and landfill elimination.
agencies.articleTitle: Material Circulation, Resource Recovery, and the Elimination of Landfills

agencies.name: National Continuity and Infrastructure Stability Authority
agencies.description: The authority maintaining minimum infrastructure standards, continuity cells, reserves, degraded-operation plans, and restoration readiness.
agencies.articleTitle: Homeland Infrastructure Stability and Civilization Continuity

agencies.name: Regional Survival Commands
agencies.description: Regional operational commands within Survival Mode for continuity, restoration, and emergency distribution.
agencies.articleTitle: National Survival Mode and the Preservation of Civil Society

agencies.name: Geographic Continuity Cells
agencies.description: Local continuity units for degraded operations, restoration sequencing, and civil-society preservation.
agencies.articleTitle: National Survival Mode and the Preservation of Civil Society

agencies.name: National Homelessness Elimination Program
agencies.description: A national program to eliminate homelessness through shelter, food, water, sanitation, medical care, and safe placement.
agencies.articleTitle: Domestic Human Security, Homelessness Elimination, and Suspension of Foreign Aid

agencies.name: National Safe Haven Program
agencies.description: A secure, clean, non-carceral housing and stabilization program for people requiring protective placement.
agencies.articleTitle: Domestic Human Security, Homelessness Elimination, and Suspension of Foreign Aid

agencies.name: National Housing Authority
agencies.description: The authority for ownership limits, lawful prices, nonprofit rent, first ownership, and commercial conversion into housing.
agencies.articleTitle: Housing Access, Limited Ownership, Nonprofit Transfer, and Income-Based Occupancy

agencies.name: National Residential Exchange
agencies.description: A housing exchange for lawful transfers, nonprofit conversion, and anti-speculation residential movement.
agencies.articleTitle: Housing Access, Limited Ownership, Nonprofit Transfer, and Income-Based Occupancy

agencies.name: National Rail Development and Mobility Authority
agencies.description: The independent authority planning corridors, state capital hubs, national rail lines, mobility access, and interoperability.
agencies.articleTitle: National Rail Revitalization, Mobility Access, and Transportation Independence

agencies.name: Rail Technology Program
agencies.description: A technology program for rail design, manufacturing, repair, control systems, safety, and continuity.
agencies.articleTitle: National Rail Revitalization, Mobility Access, and Transportation Independence

agencies.name: Retired Veteran School Protection Corps
agencies.description: A school protection corps using qualified retired veterans under training, oversight, and civilian educational authority.
agencies.articleTitle: National Education, Child Development, Productive Learning, and School Security

agencies.name: National Health Intelligence Network
agencies.description: A de-identified medical data network with state nodes, national analysis, independent backups, and research protections.
agencies.articleTitle: National Health Intelligence, De-Identified Medical Data, and Preventive Care

agencies.name: National Health Data Council
agencies.description: An oversight council including clinicians, mental-health professionals, patient advocates, data experts, and public representatives.
agencies.articleTitle: National Health Intelligence, De-Identified Medical Data, and Preventive Care

agencies.name: National Circular Sanitation Authority
agencies.description: The authority leading the transition from water-carried sewage to circular sanitation, water conservation, and resource recovery.
agencies.articleTitle: National Circular Sanitation, Water Conservation, and Elimination of Conventional Water-Carried Sewage
